hewitt2019control.title: Designing and Interpreting Probes with Control Tasks
hewitt2019control.local_pdf: papers/Designing and Interpreting Probes with Control Tasks-D19-1275.pdf
hewitt2019control.local_md: papers/Designing and Interpreting Probes with Control Tasks-D19-1275.md
hewitt2019control.role: Establishes probe accuracy != causal use (foundational). Control tasks show probes can achieve high accuracy for uninformative reasons.

elazar2021amnesic.title: Amnesic Probing: Behavioral Explanation with Amnesic Counterfactuals
elazar2021amnesic.authors: Yanai Elazar, Shauli Ravfogel, Alon Jacovi, Yoav Goldberg
elazar2021amnesic.role: Amnesic counterfactuals for causal probing. Shows probes can find info model doesn't use.

kumar2022probes.title: Probing Classifiers are Unreliable for Concept Removal and Detection
kumar2022probes.authors: Abhinav Kumar, Chenhao Tan, Amit Sharma
kumar2022probes.role: Theory+empirics: probes unreliable for concept removal. Concept removal setting, not LLM steering.

hase2023localization.title: Does Localization Inform Editing? Surprising Differences in Causality-Based Localization vs. Knowledge Editing in Language Models
hase2023localization.authors: Peter Hase, Mohit Bansal, Been Kim, Asma Ghandeharioun
hase2023localization.role: Strongest precedent for localization->control break. Knowledge editing, not activation steering.

li2023iti.title: Inference-Time Intervention: Eliciting Truthful Answers from a Language Model
li2023iti.authors: Kenneth Li, Oam Patel, Fernanda Viégas, Hanspeter Pfister, Martin Wattenberg
li2023iti.local_pdf: papers/Inference-Time Intervention:Eliciting Truthful Answers from a Language Model2306.03341v6.pdf
li2023iti.local_md: papers/Inference-Time Intervention:Eliciting Truthful Answers from a Language Model2306.03341v6.md
li2023iti.role: Seed lineage. MC-vs-generation mismatch. Probe-weight direction != best steering direction.

tan2024steering.title: Analyzing the Generalization and Reliability of Steering Vectors
tan2024steering.authors: Daniel Tan, David Chanin, Aengus Lynch, Dimitrios Kanoulas, Brooks Paige, Adria Garriga-Alonso, Robert Kirk
tan2024steering.role: Per-sample variance and brittleness of steering vectors. Reliability evidence.

pres2024reliable.title: Towards Reliable Evaluation of Behavior Steering Interventions in LLMs
pres2024reliable.authors: Itamar Pres, Laura Ruis, Ekdeep Singh Lubana, David Krueger
pres2024reliable.role: Format dependence of steering evaluation. MC vs generation gap in steering context.

bhalla2024unifying.title: Towards Unifying Interpretability and Control: Evaluation via Intervention
bhalla2024unifying.authors: Usha Bhalla, Suraj Srinivas, Asma Ghandeharioun, Himabindu Lakkaraju
bhalla2024unifying.role: Explicitly frames predict/control discrepancy. Several mech-interp methods underperform prompting.

souly2024strongreject.title: A StrongREJECT for Empty Jailbreaks
souly2024strongreject.role: Binary vs graded jailbreak evaluation. Establishes generic measurement fragility for safety eval.

arditi2024refusal.title: Refusal in Language Models Is Mediated by a Single Direction
arditi2024refusal.authors: Andy Arditi, Oscar Balcells Obeso, Aaquib Syed, Daniel Paleka, Nina Rimsky, Wes Gurnee, Neel Nanda
arditi2024refusal.role: Strong positive counterexample. Single-axis refusal steering works well.

ming2025faitheval.title: FaithEval: Can Your Language Model Stay Faithful to Context, Even If 'The Moon is Made of Marshmallows'
ming2025faitheval.role: Benchmark grounding for anchor case study 1 (FaithEval). Diagnostic faithfulness benchmark.

arad2025saes.title: SAEs Are Good for Steering – If You Select the Right Features
arad2025saes.authors: Dana Arad, Aaron Mueller, Yonatan Belinkov
arad2025saes.local_pdf: papers/SAEs Are Good for Steering – If You Select the Right Features-2505.20063v2.pdf
arad2025saes.local_md: papers/SAEs Are Good for Steering – If You Select the Right Features-2505.20063v2.md
arad2025saes.role: Strongest near-direct prior. Input-vs-output feature selection for SAEs. ~2-3x steering improvement with output-score filtering.

wu2025axbench.title: AxBench: Steering LLMs? Even Simple Baselines Outperform Sparse Autoencoders
wu2025axbench.role: Separates detection from steering. Simple baselines outperform SAEs on steering. Synthetic concepts on Gemma-2.
wu2025axbench.notes: Downloaded via arXiv mirror and converted to markdown

chen2025safer.title: Safer or Luckier? LLMs as Safety Evaluators Are Not Robust to Artifacts
chen2025safer.role: LLM judge artifacts in safety evaluation. Establishes generic measurement fragility.

huang2025guidedbench.title: GuidedBench: Equipping Jailbreak Evaluation with Guidelines
huang2025guidedbench.authors: Ruixuan Huang, Xunguang Wang, Zongjie Li, Daoyuan Wu, Shuai Wang
huang2025guidedbench.role: Judge guidelines reduce variance. Supports measurement fragility claim.

eiras2025judge.title: Know Thy Judge: On the Robustness Meta-Evaluation of LLM Safety Judges
eiras2025judge.authors: Francisco Eiras, Eliott Zemour, Eric Lin, Vaikkunth Mugunthan
eiras2025judge.role: Judge-robustness meta-evaluation. Presentation changes and output-level attacks can sharply change false-negative behavior.

gao2025hneurons.title: H-Neurons: On the Existence, Impact, and Origin of Hallucination-Associated Neurons in LLMs
gao2025hneurons.authors: Cheng Gao, Huimin Chen, Chaojun Xiao, Zhiyi Chen, Zhiyuan Liu, Maosong Sun
gao2025hneurons.role: Seed paper. <0.1% neurons detect hallucination; interventions modulate over-compliance. Positive counterexample.
gao2025hneurons.notes: Already have markdown — seed lineage paper

dunefsky2025oneshot.title: One-shot Optimized Steering Vectors Mediate Safety-relevant Behaviors in LLMs
dunefsky2025oneshot.role: Positive counterexample. Optimized steering vectors work for safety-relevant behaviors.

nguyen2025matsteer.title: Multi-Attribute Steering of Language Models via Targeted Intervention
nguyen2025matsteer.authors: Duy Nguyen, Archiki Prasad, Elias Stengel-Eskin, Mohit Bansal
nguyen2025matsteer.role: Positive counterexample with multi-attribute steering. More structured approach works.

opielka2026causality.title: Causality ≠ Invariance: Function and Concept Vectors in LLMs
opielka2026causality.authors: Gustaw Opiełka, Hannes Rosenbusch, Claire E. Stevenson
opielka2026causality.role: Format-dependent vectors. Orthogonal representations across MC/open-ended. Strong support for surface-locality.

yap2026behavioral.title: Behavioral Steering in a 35B MoE Language Model via SAE-Decoded Probe Vectors: One Agency Axis, Not Five Traits
yap2026behavioral.role: Context for probe-vector steering in MoE. Single-author preprint, downweight.
yap2026behavioral.notes: Optional — single author preprint, low priority

ban2026bridge.title: Completing Missing Annotation: Multi-Agent Debate for Accurate and Scalable Relevant Assessment for IR Benchmarks (BRIDGE)
ban2026bridge.role: IR/RAG relevance benchmark. NOT direct activation-steering evidence. Only indirect externality framing.
ban2026bridge.notes: Optional — not direct evidence, only namesake overlap with our bridge benchmark

you2026spherical.title: Spherical Steering: Geometry-Aware Activation Rotation for Language Models
you2026spherical.authors: Zejia You, Chunyuan Deng, Hanjie Chen
you2026spherical.role: Positive counterexample. Claims improved MC AND maintained generation. Contradicts MC-up/gen-down narrative.
you2026spherical.notes: Optional — actually contradicts one report's interpretation

surgical2025causal.title: Surgical Activation Steering via Generative Causal Mediation
surgical2025causal.local_pdf: papers/Surgical Activation Steering via Generative Causal Mediation-2602.16080v1.pdf
surgical2025causal.local_md: papers/Surgical Activation Steering via Generative Causal Mediation-2602.16080v1.md
surgical2025causal.role: Causal mediation for steering. Context for D7 causal selector comparison.
